pr.title: Use symbol names from Zoids VS debug elf
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Applied symbol names sourced from the Zoids VS debug ELF across 51 files, mainly Dolphin DVD and VI/video-related assembly, with smaller updates in CARD, GX, PAD, sysdolphin baselib, and a few Melee/game source/header references. The PR was primarily a naming pass: anonymous func_/lbl_ symbols and SDA globals were replaced with meaningful SDK-like names such as __CARDRead, __CARDWrite, DVDLowRead, stateReadingFST, cbForStateReadingFST, stateTimeout, stateGettingError, CategorizeError, bootInfo, tmpBuffer, DummyCommandBlock, FatalErrorFlag, Canceling, CancelCallback, LastError, and NumInternalRetry. Evidence indicates no behavioral rewrite; changes are balanced additions/deletions and mostly rename callsites/labels.
what_changed: Renamed CARD read/write internals from address-style names to SDK-style private names: func_803547FC became __CARDRead and func_8035493C became __CARDWrite.
Updated all visible CARD callsites in CARDBlock.s, CARDDir.s, CARDFormat.s, CARDMount.s, CARDRead.s, and CARDWrite.s to call __CARDRead or __CARDWrite instead of the old func_ names.

Renamed many DVD state-machine labels and globals in asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s, including stateReadingFST, cbForStateReadingFST, cbForStateError, stateTimeout, stateGettingError, CategorizeError, bootInfo, tmpBuffer, DummyCommandBlock, LastState, FatalErrorFlag, Canceling, CancelCallback, LastError, and NumInternalRetry.
Renamed low-level DVD callsites from func_ names to recognizable DVDLow* names in the excerpt, including DVDLowRead, DVDLowRequestError, and DVDLowStopMotor.

Applied a broad symbol-name cleanup across DVD, VI/video, CARD, GX, PAD, sysdolphin baselib, and some Melee assembly/source/header files, with 748 additions and 753 deletions across 51 files, consistent with mass renaming rather than new logic.

Touched headers such as include/dolphin/vi/vi.h, include/dolphin/pad/pad.h, include/functions.h, and src/dolphin/gx/GX_unknown_001.h to keep declarations or referenced names aligned with renamed symbols.
smart_moves: Used an external debug ELF as an evidence source for symbol names instead of guessing from local control flow alone.
Propagated renamed symbols through both definitions and callsites, preventing stale references to func_803547FC, func_8035493C, and related lbl_ names.
Kept instruction bytes and addresses intact in assembly comments while changing only labels/symbol operands, making the renaming reviewable and preserving matching confidence.
Focused the largest rename set on Dolphin SDK-like subsystems where cross-title debug symbols are especially reusable: DVD, CARD, VI, GX, and PAD.
Converted opaque SDA labels into semantic global names in DVD code, which improves future C decompilation by exposing state-machine variables and error/cancel bookkeeping.
decomp_lessons: When a related game has a debug ELF, its SDK/library symbol names can be imported into Melee to replace func_/lbl_ placeholders, especially for shared Dolphin SDK and baselib code.
A reliable naming pass should rename definitions and every branch/call/reference in the same PR; leaving mixed old/new names makes future matching and C translation harder.
SDK-style private symbols may legitimately use double underscores, as shown by __CARDRead and __CARDWrite.
DVD state machines benefit from naming callbacks and state handlers by role, for example stateReadingFST, cbForStateReadingFST, cbForStateError, stateTimeout, and stateGettingError.

SDA/global labels should be renamed when their role is known, because names like FatalErrorFlag, Canceling, CancelCallback, LastError, and NumInternalRetry communicate invariants that are otherwise hidden in raw lbl_804Dxxxx names.
Balanced add/delete diffs in assembly naming PRs are a useful signal that the change is symbol metadata rather than behavior, but exact correctness still depends on the external symbol source.
naming_conventions: Use Dolphin SDK/private naming where supported by external evidence: __CARDRead, __CARDWrite, DVDLowRead, DVDLowRequestError, DVDLowStopMotor.
Use state* for DVD state handlers: stateReadingFST, stateTimeout, stateGettingError.
Use cbFor* for callbacks tied to state-machine phases: cbForStateReadingFST, cbForStateGettingError, cbForStateError.
Use descriptive PascalCase or mixed-case globals for SDK-style state variables: DummyCommandBlock, FatalErrorFlag, Canceling, CancelCallback, LastError, NumInternalRetry, LastState.
Prefer semantically meaningful data names such as bootInfo and tmpBuffer over raw SDA or BSS labels.
Retain exact capitalization seen in sourced SDK/debug symbols; examples include FST in stateReadingFST and DVDLow* prefixes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly-only rename pass: replace .global labels, local labels, branch labels, and symbol operands while preserving instruction encodings and address comments.
Update branch-and-link operands to point at renamed symbols without changing machine code, e.g. bl func_8035493C to bl __CARDWrite and bl func_803547FC to bl __CARDRead.
For state-machine code, rename both the handler label and the address materialization operands using @ha/@l pairs, e.g. lbl_80337EE4 to stateReadingFST.
Rename SDA references consistently in load/store operands, e.g. lbl_804D72A0@sda21 to bootInfo@sda21 and lbl_804D72CC@sda21 to NumInternalRetry@sda21.
Keep large mechanical rename patches constrained to symbol identity so they do not interfere with later function matching or C conversion.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states: "Mostly renaming dolphin dvd and video code currently"; there is no explicit reviewer rationale beyond the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that converted renamed DVD symbols such as stateReadingFST or CategorizeError into C.
Find subsequent uses of Zoids VS debug ELF names in Dolphin SDK or sysdolphin baselib files.
Audit whether __CARDRead and __CARDWrite declarations were later moved from assembly-only symbols into headers or C source.
Search for remaining func_ or lbl_ names near DVDLowRead, DVDLowRequestError, and DVDLowStopMotor that may have matching debug ELF names.
Compare the renamed VI/video symbols in asm/dolphin/vi/vi.s and include/dolphin/vi/vi.h against later decompilation work.
